(c) MnO2 is oxidized to MnCl2 & HCl is reduced to Cl2

subject: science
question:
A clear solution of slaked lime is made by dissolving Ca(OH)2 in an excess of water. This solution is left exposed to air. The solution slowly goes milky as a faint white precipitate forms. Explain why a faint white precipitate forms, support your response with the help of a chemical equation.
OR
Keerti added dilute Hydrochloric acid to four metals and recorded her observations as shown in the table given below:
Metal | Gas Evolved
Copper | Yes
Iron | Yes
Magnesium | No
Zinc | Yes
Select the correct observation(s) and give chemical equation(s) of the reaction involved.
answer:
Ca(OH)₂ reacts with CO₂ in the atmosphere to form CaCO₃ (white precipitate) and water.
Ca(OH)₂ + CO₂ → CaCO₃ + H₂O
OR
Copper does NOT react with HCl (incorrect observation). Correct ones: Fe + 2HCl → FeCl₂ + H₂; Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂. Magnesium does react with HCl (also incorrect in table).
chapter: Acids, Bases and Salts / Metals and Non-metals
topic: Reaction of Ca(OH)₂ with CO₂; Metal-acid reactions

subject: science
question: How is the mode of action in beating of the heart different from reflex actions? Give four examples.
answer: Beating of the heart is involuntary and controlled by the sino-atrial node (pace-maker) — it does not require a stimulus and the nerve impulse does not travel to the brain. Reflex actions are automatic responses to a specific stimulus that travel through a reflex arc (receptor → sensory nerve → spinal cord → motor nerve → effector) without conscious brain involvement but do require a stimulus. Four examples of reflex actions: (i) withdrawal of hand on touching a hot object, (ii) blinking of eyes on a sudden bright light, (iii) sneezing/coughing, (iv) salivation on seeing food.
topic: Nervous system — reflex actions vs involuntary actions

subject: science
question: Patients whose gallbladder are removed are recommended to eat less oily food. Why?
answer: The gallbladder stores bile produced by the liver. Bile helps in emulsification of fats (breaks large fat globules into smaller ones, increasing surface area for enzyme action). Without the gallbladder, bile cannot be stored and released in adequate quantity when fatty food is consumed, so emulsification of fats will be negligible/affected, making fat digestion slow and incomplete. Hence such patients are advised to eat less oily food.
topic: Digestion — role of bile and gallbladder

subject: science
question: Name the substances other than water, that are reabsorbed during urine formation. What are the two parameters that decide the amount of water that is reabsorbed in the kidney?
answer: Substances reabsorbed (other than water): glucose, amino acids, salts (any two). The two parameters that decide the amount of water reabsorbed are: (1) how much excess water there is in the body, and (2) how much dissolved waste there is to be excreted.
topic: Excretion — urine formation and reabsorption

subject: science
question:
State the phenomena observed in the above diagram. Explain with reference to the diagram, which of the two lights mentioned above will have the higher wavelength?
OR
How will you use two identical prisms so that a narrow beam of white light incident on one prism emerges out of the second prism as white light? Draw the diagram.
answer:
Dispersion — the splitting of white light into seven colours (VIBGYOR) on passing through a prism. Velocity is directly proportional to wavelength (at constant frequency). Yellow light travels faster through the prism than blue light, so yellow has a greater wavelength than blue.
OR
Place the two prisms with their refracting edges parallel but inverted (angles of deflection equal and opposite). The first prism splits white light into its spectrum; the second prism recombines the colours so that white light emerges as the final output.
chapter: Light — Reflection and Refraction / The Human Eye and the Colourful World
topic: Dispersion of light through a prism

subject: science
question: A lot of waste is generated in neighborhood. However, almost all of it is biodegradable. What impact will it have on the environment or human health?
answer: Even biodegradable waste can be harmful when generated in excess. Its decomposition is a slow process that produces foul smell and harmful gases. Large amounts of decomposing waste can become a breeding ground for pathogens (bacteria, flies, mosquitoes), creating unhygienic conditions that can spread diseases and adversely affect human health and the local environment.
topic: Biodegradable waste and its environmental impact

subject: science
question: Identify the types of reaction mentioned above in (i) and (ii). Give one example for each type in the form of a balanced chemical equation.
answer:
(i) Displacement reaction. Example: Fe(s) + CuSO₄(aq) → FeSO₄(aq) + Cu(s)
(ii) Double displacement reaction. Example: Na₂SO₄(aq) + BaCl₂(aq) → BaSO₄(s) + 2NaCl(aq)
topic: Types of chemical reactions — displacement and double displacement

subject: science
question:
(a) Identify the gases evolved at the anode and cathode in the above experimental set up.
(b) Name the process that occurs. Why is it called so?
(c) Illustrate the reaction of the process with the help of a chemical equation.
answer:
(a) Anode: Chlorine (Cl₂); Cathode: Hydrogen (H₂)
(b) Chlor-alkali process — named so because the products are chlorine (chlor) gas and an alkali (NaOH), along with hydrogen gas.
(c) 2NaCl(aq) + 2H₂O(l) → 2NaOH(aq) + Cl₂(g) + H₂(g)
topic: Electrolysis of brine — chlor-alkali process

subject: science
question:
The leaves of a plant were covered with aluminium foil, how would it affect the physiology of the plant?
OR
How is lymph an important fluid involved in transportation? If lymphatic vessels get blocked, how would it affect the human body? Elaborate.
answer:
Covering leaves with aluminium foil would: (1) Stop photosynthesis — no glucose would be produced as light cannot reach chloroplasts. (2) Stop respiration via stomata — no O₂ intake for aerobic respiration. (3) Stop transpiration — no water loss means no transpirational pull, so upward movement of water and minerals from roots would stop. (4) Temperature regulation of the leaf surface would be affected.
OR
Lymph: (1) carries digested and absorbed fat from the intestine (lacteals in villi) to the bloodstream. (2) Drains excess tissue fluid (extracellular fluid) back into the blood. Blockage of lymphatic vessels would lead to water retention (oedema/swelling) in the tissues and poor absorption of fats, affecting overall circulation and nutrition.
topic: Photosynthesis / Transportation — lymph

subject: science
question:
Rohit wants to have an erect image of an object using a converging mirror of focal length 40 cm.
(a) Specify the range of distance where the object can be placed in front of the mirror. Justify.
(b) Draw a ray diagram to show image formation in this case.
(c) State one use of the mirror based on the above kind of image formation.
answer:
(a) The object must be placed between 0 and 40 cm (between the pole P and the focus F) in front of the mirror. Only then is the image virtual, erect and magnified.
(b) Ray diagram: object between F and P → two rays drawn (one parallel to principal axis reflects through F; one directed towards centre of curvature reflects back on itself) → image is virtual, erect and magnified, formed behind the mirror.
(c) Used as a shaving mirror / dentist's mirror (to obtain an enlarged, erect image).
topic: Concave mirror — image formation between F and P

subject: science
question:
(a) A lens of focal length 5 cm is being used by Debashree in the laboratory as a magnifying glass. Her least distance of distinct vision is 25 cm.
(i) What is the magnification obtained by using the glass?
(ii) She keeps a book at a distance 10 cm from her eyes and tries to read. She is unable to read. What is the reason for this?
(b) Ravi kept a book at a distance of 10 cm from the eyes of his friend Hari. Hari is not able to read anything written in the book. Give reasons for this?
answer:
(a)(i) Magnification m = 1 + D/f = 1 + 25/5 = 6
(a)(ii) The least distance of distinct vision is 25 cm; the book is placed at only 10 cm which is less than 25 cm (the near point), so the eye cannot form a clear image.
(b) The least distance of distinct vision is 25 cm. Since Ravi has placed the book at 10 cm, which is less than the minimum distance (25 cm) required for distinct vision, Hari cannot read it clearly.
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World
topic: Magnifying glass; least distance of distinct vision

subject: science
question:
A student fixes a white sheet of paper on a drawing board. He places a bar magnet in the centre and sprinkles some iron filings uniformly around the bar magnet. Then he taps gently and observes that iron filings arrange themselves in a certain pattern.
(a) Why do iron filings arrange themselves in a particular pattern?
(b) Which physical quantity is indicated by the pattern of field lines around the bar magnet?
(c) State any two properties of magnetic field lines.
OR
A compass needle is placed near a current carrying wire. State your observations for the following cases and give reasons for the same in each case:
(a) Magnitude of electric current in wire is increased.
(b) The compass needle is displaced away from the wire.
answer:
(a) Iron filings behave like tiny magnets in the magnetic field. The magnetic force on them makes them rotate and align along the direction of the field lines.
(b) The magnetic field (strength and direction) produced by the bar magnet.
(c) Any two: (i) Magnetic field lines never intersect each other. (ii) Magnetic field lines are closed continuous curves. (iii) They emerge from the north pole and enter the south pole outside the magnet. (iv) Closer field lines indicate stronger field.
OR
(a) Deflection of compass needle increases — because the magnetic field of a current-carrying conductor is directly proportional to the current flowing through it.
(b) Deflection of the compass needle decreases — because the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the perpendicular distance from the wire.
topic: Magnetic field lines — properties; Oersted's experiment

subject: science
question: Why is damage to the ozone layer a cause for concern? What are its causes and what steps are being taken to limit this damage?
answer:
Concern: The ozone layer shields the Earth's surface from harmful UV radiations from the Sun. Depletion of the ozone layer allows more UV radiation to reach the Earth, which can cause skin cancer in humans and is harmful to other life forms.
Causes: Synthetic chemicals like chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) — used as refrigerants and in fire extinguishers — are the primary cause.
Steps taken: Many developing and developed countries have signed agreements under the UNEP (United Nations Environment Programme) to freeze or limit production and usage of CFCs at 1986 levels (Montreal Protocol).
topic: Ozone layer depletion

subject: science
question:
Shristi heated Ethanol with a compound A in presence of a few drops of concentrated sulphuric acid and observed a sweet smelling compound B is formed. When B is treated with sodium hydroxide it gives back Ethanol and a compound C.
(a) Identify A and C
(b) Give one use each of compounds A and B.
(c) Write the chemical reactions involved and name the reactions.
OR
(a) What is the role of concentrated Sulphuric acid when it is heated with Ethanol at 443 K. Give the reaction involved.
(b) Reshu by mistake forgot to label the two test tubes containing Ethanol and Ethanoic acid. Suggest an experiment to identify the substances correctly? Illustrate the reactions with the help of chemical equations
answer:
(a) A = Ethanoic acid (CH₃COOH); C = Sodium ethanoate (CH₃COONa)
(b) Use of A: dilute solution used as vinegar in cooking / as a preservative in pickles. Use of B (ethyl ethanoate): making perfumes, flavouring agent.
(c) Esterification: CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH → (conc. H₂SO₄) → CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O
Saponification: CH₃COOC₂H₅ + NaOH → CH₃COONa + C₂H₅OH
OR
(a) Concentrated H₂SO₄ acts as a dehydrating agent. C₂H₅OH → (conc. H₂SO₄, 443K) → C₂H₄ + H₂O
(b) Add sodium carbonate / sodium bicarbonate to both test tubes. Ethanol will not react; Ethanoic acid gives brisk effervescence (CO₂).
2CH₃COOH + Na₂CO₃ → 2CH₃COONa + H₂O + CO₂
OR: CH₃COOH + NaHCO₃ → CH₃COONa + H₂O + CO₂
topic: Esterification and saponification; dehydration of ethanol

subject: science
question:
(a) Why is it not possible to reconstruct the whole organism from a fragment in complex multicellular organisms?
(b) Sexual maturation of reproductive tissues and organs are necessary link for reproduction. Elucidate.
OR
(a) How are variations useful for species if there is drastic alteration in the niches?
(b) Explain how the uterus and placenta provide necessary conditions for proper growth and development of the embryo after implantation?
answer:
(a) Complex multicellular organisms are not a random collection of cells; specialised cells are organised as tissues, and tissues are organised into organs placed at definite positions in the body. Therefore, cell-by-cell division (regeneration from a fragment) would be impractical.
(b) Sexual maturation is necessary because specialised cells (germ cells/gametes) are required for sexual reproduction. The body must grow to adult size, general body growth slows, and reproductive tissues begin to mature during puberty. Physical changes appear (in girls: breast development, menstruation; in boys: facial hair, voice change), enabling reproduction.
OR
(a) If niches are drastically altered, a population without variation could be wiped out. Variations in some individuals give them a chance to survive altered conditions. Variation is thus useful for the survival of species over time.
(b) After implantation: the uterine lining thickens and is richly supplied with blood to nourish the embryo. The placenta is embedded in the uterine wall and has villi on the embryo's side surrounded by blood spaces on the mother's side, providing large surface area for exchange of glucose, oxygen, and waste products between mother and embryo. The embryo is finally expelled by rhythmic contractions of the uterus.
chapter: How do Organisms Reproduce? / Heredity and Evolution
topic: Regeneration in multicellular organisms; puberty; variations; placenta

subject: science
question:
The diagram above is a schematic diagram of a household circuit. The house shown in the above diagram has 5 usable spaces where electrical connections are made. For this house, the mains have a voltage of 220 V and the net current coming from the mains is 22A.
(a) What is the mode of connection to all the spaces in the house from the mains?
(b) The spaces 5 and 4 have the same resistance and spaces 3 and 2 have respective resistances of 20Ω and 30Ω. Space 1 has a resistance double that of space 5. What is the net resistance for space 5.
(c) What is the current in space 3?
(d) What should be placed between the main connection and the rest of the house's electrical appliances to save them from accidental high electric current?
answer:
(a) All spaces are connected in parallel.
(b) Total resistance = V/I = 220/22 = 10 Ω. Let resistance of space 5 (and 4) = R; space 1 = 2R; space 2 = 30 Ω; space 3 = 20 Ω.
1/10 = 1/R + 1/R + 1/(2R) + 1/20 + 1/30
1/10 = 5/(2R) + 1/20 + 1/30
1/10 - 1/20 - 1/30 = 5/(2R)
(6-3-2)/60 = 5/(2R) → 1/60 = 5/(2R) → R = 150 Ω. Net resistance of space 5 = 150 Ω.
(c) Current in space 3 = V/R₃ = 220/20 = 11 A.
(d) A fuse (or MCB — Miniature Circuit Breaker) should be placed between the main connection and the electrical appliances.
topic: Parallel circuit — household wiring, resistance calculation, safety devices

subject: science
question:
Two students decided to investigate the effect of water and air on iron object under identical experimental conditions. They measured the mass of each object before placing it partially immersed in 10 ml of water. After a few days, the object were removed, dried and their masses were measured. The table shows their results.
Student | Object | Mass of Object before Rusting in g | Mass of the coated object in g
A | Nail | 3.0 | 3.15
B | Thin plate | 6.0 | 6.33
(a) What might be the reason for the varied observations of the two students?
(b) In another set up the students coated iron nails with zinc metal and noted that, iron nails coated with zinc prevents rusting. They also observed that zinc initially acts as a physical barrier, but an extra advantage of using zinc is that it continues to prevent rusting even if the layer of zinc is damaged. Name this process of rust prevention and give any two other methods to prevent rusting.
OR
(b) In which of the following applications of Iron, rusting will occur most? Support your answer with valid reason.
A - Iron Bucket electroplated with Zinc
B - Electricity cables having iron wires covered with aluminium
C - Iron hinges on a gate
D - Painted iron fence
answer:
(a) Rusting occurs in both cases, increasing the mass. The thin plate (B) has a greater surface area exposed to water and air compared to the nail (A), so the extent of rusting (and mass gain) is more for B.
(b) Galvanisation. Other methods to prevent rusting: painting, oiling/greasing, alloying, chromium plating (any two).
OR
(b) C — Iron hinges on a gate. Iron is directly in contact with both atmospheric oxygen and moisture/water vapour without any protective coating, which are the two essential conditions for rusting.
topic: Corrosion — rusting of iron, prevention methods, galvanisation

subject: science
question:
Pooja has green eyes while her parents and brother have black eyes. Pooja's husband Ravi has black eyes while his mother has green eyes and father has black eyes.
(a) On the basis of the above given information, is the green eye colour a dominant or recessive trait? Justify your answer.
(b) What is the possible genetic makeup of Pooja's brother's eye colour?
(c) What is the probability that the offspring of Pooja and Ravi will have green eyes? Also, show the inheritance of eye colour in the offspring with the help of a suitable cross.
OR
(d) 50% of the offspring of Pooja's brother are green eyed. With help of cross show how this is possible.
answer:
(a) Green eye colour is recessive, because it is not expressed in the parents (who carry but do not show it) yet appears in Pooja. A recessive trait expresses only in homozygous condition (bb).
(b) Pooja's brother has black eyes; his possible genotypes are BB or Bb (since parents must both be Bb to produce green-eyed Pooja).
(c) Pooja = bb; Ravi's mother is green-eyed (bb) so Ravi must be Bb. Cross: bb × Bb → Bb, bb, Bb, bb → 50% probability of green eyes (bb).
OR
(d) Pooja's brother is Bb (heterozygous) and his partner is bb (green-eyed). Cross Bb × bb → Bb, bb, Bb, bb → 50% offspring are green-eyed.
topic: Mendelian genetics — dominant/recessive traits, monohybrid cross

subject: science
question:
The above images are that of a specialized slide projector. Slides are small transparencies mounted in sturdy frames ideally suited to magnification and projection, since they have a very high resolution and a high image quality. There is a tray where the slides are to be put into a particular orientation so that the viewers can see the enlarged erect images of the transparent slides. This means that the slides will have to be inserted upside down in the projector tray.
To show her students the images of insects that she investigated in the lab, Mrs. Iyer brought a slide projector. Her slide projector produced a 500 times enlarged and inverted image of a slide on a screen 10 m away.
(a) Based on the text and data given in the above paragraph, what kind of lens must the slide projector have?
(b) If v is the symbol used for image distance and u for object distance then with one reason state what will be the sign for v/u in the given case?
(c) A slide projector has a convex lens with a focal length of 20 cm. The slide is placed upside down 21 cm from the lens. How far away should the screen be placed from the slide projector's lens so that the slide is in focus?
OR
(c) When a slide is placed 15 cm behind the lens in the projector, an image is formed 3 m in front of the lens. If the focal length of the lens is 14 cm, draw a ray diagram to show image formation. (not to scale)
answer:
(a) Convex lens (converging lens).
(b) The sign of v/u is negative. The image is real and inverted; by sign convention, v is positive (image on opposite side) and u is negative (object on same side as incoming light), so v/u is negative.
(c) Using lens formula: 1/f = 1/v − 1/u; f = 20 cm, u = −21 cm
1/20 = 1/v − 1/(−21) = 1/v + 1/21
1/v = 1/20 − 1/21 = (21−20)/420 = 1/420
v = 420 cm
The screen should be placed 420 cm (4.2 m) from the lens.
OR
(c) Ray diagram (not to scale): object at 15 cm, f = 14 cm (object just beyond F); image is real, inverted, highly magnified, formed at 3 m (300 cm) on the other side of the lens.
topic: Convex lens — image formation, lens formula, sign convention
